source_alignment.subject: Science
source_alignment.source_statement: Working scientifically: asking questions, making predictions, planning investigations, identifying variables, recording observations and measurements, evaluating data and presenting reasoned conclusions.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This Year 7 pack turns investigation planning into an accessible planner with prediction-observation-explanation cycles and explicit variable control.

objective.child_goal: I can plan a fair investigation and explain what the evidence shows.
objective.statement: Ask testable questions, make reasoned predictions, identify independent, dependent and control variables, plan safe data collection, and write conclusions linked to evidence.
misconceptions: Thinks a fair test means everything stays the same, including the variable being tested.
Writes conclusions from guesses or preferences rather than evidence.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The one thing you deliberately change.
vocabulary.audio_script: The independent variable is the one thing you change on purpose.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A single highlighted slider labelled change.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The thing you measure or observe to see what happens.
vocabulary.audio_script: The dependent variable is what you measure or observe.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A measuring tool connected to the result.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A thing you keep the same so the test is fair.
vocabulary.audio_script: Control variables are kept the same so the investigation is fair.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Locked cards around the investigation planner.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which question could be tested with measurements?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Separate scientific testable questions from opinions or impossible questions.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A testable question can be investigated using observations or measurements.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Question cards sort into testable, opinion and too broad.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Use the investigation planner to choose what to change, measure and keep the same.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce variable roles as the backbone of fair investigation planning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Change one thing, measure one result and keep important conditions the same.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three planner columns labelled change, measure and keep the same.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Plan a fair test for which paper towel absorbs the most water.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model variable identification, safe method order and measurement choice.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: We change the paper towel type, measure the volume of water absorbed and keep the water volume and towel size the same.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Planner fills with variable cards and a simple measurement table.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Predict, observe the trial data, then explain whether the prediction was supported.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect predictions and conclusions to evidence rather than correctness or preference.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A prediction can be supported or not supported by evidence. Either result can teach us something.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Prediction, observation and explanation cards link to a data table.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: In a fair test, should the independent variable stay the same?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Repair the misconception that fair means every variable stays unchanged.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: In a fair test, the independent variable changes on purpose. Control variables stay the same.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: One change card unlocks while control cards stay locked.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Mira how to improve a conclusion so it uses evidence.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I wrote what I thought would happen. Can you help me use the measurements?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Companion conclusion is revised by dragging evidence from a table into a sentence frame.

manipulatives.purpose: Help learners plan fair investigations by selecting variables, method steps, safety notes, repeats, measurements and evidence-linked conclusions.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners select cards for independent, dependent and control variables, order method steps, choose a table layout, run a safe simulated trial and complete a prediction-observation-explanation cycle.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Supports keyboard and switch access, large card targets, no time pressure, low-sensory static planner mode, spoken card descriptions, printable method strips and reduced-motion data reveals.

manipulatives.purpose: Turn evaluation into an investigation audit where learners identify which claims are supported, uncertain or contradicted by the data.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners inspect methods and results, spend audit markers on repeatability, anomalies and control issues, then revise an overconfident conclusion.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Offers keyboard and switch audit markers, spoken tables, plain-language confidence labels, a static checklist and no speed scoring.

practice.interleaving_notes: Use investigation planning across biology, chemistry and physics packs; revisit variable identification before graphing or practical simulations.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Identify independent, dependent and control variables in familiar investigations.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: investigation question + variable cards + role categories + feedback
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use one clear independent variable and avoid ambiguous dual-purpose measurements.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build fair, safe method plans with variables, repeats and measurement tables.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: testable question + planner cards + ordered method + safety and repeat prompts
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid practical instructions involving hazardous materials; simulations should stay classroom-safe.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Link predictions, observations and explanations using simulated evidence.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: prediction stem + small data set + supported/not supported explanation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Emphasise that unsupported predictions are not failure when evidence is used well.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose conclusions that cite the relevant measurements and avoid overclaiming.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: data table + conclusion choices + evidence sentence frame
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Conclusions should use comparative data and acknowledge anomalies where supplied.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain variable and evidence vocabulary across later science topics.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short retrieval prompt + one role decision or evidence decision
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep prompts concise for warm-up use and provide text-only alternatives.

body.prompt: A pupil tests which paper towel absorbs the most water. Sort the variables.

value: independent variable: type of paper towel
dependent variable: volume of water absorbed
control variable: size of paper towel piece, starting volume of water

hints: What is measured to compare the result?
question_variants.explanation: The paper towel type is changed, the water absorbed is measured, and towel size plus starting water volume are kept the same.

body.prompt: Plan a fair test for how ramp height affects the distance a toy car travels.
planner_cards: change both car and ramp height

hints: Change only the variable you are testing.
Repeats help make the results more reliable.
question_variants.explanation: A fair plan changes ramp height, measures distance travelled, keeps other important conditions the same and repeats measurements.

body.prompt: Predict whether seeds with more water will germinate faster, observe the data, then choose the best explanation.
body.prediction: Seeds with more water will germinate faster.
body.observation: Seeds with a small measured amount of water germinated in 4 days; flooded seeds did not germinate in 7 days.
choices: The prediction was not fully supported; too much water may stop seeds getting enough air.
The prediction is proved because more water is always better.
The observation does not matter because predictions should not change.
The independent variable was germination time.

expected_answer.value: The prediction was not fully supported; too much water may stop seeds getting enough air.

hints: Compare the prediction with both observations.
It is scientific to say a prediction was not supported when the evidence shows that.
question_variants.explanation: The evidence suggests that more water is not always better. A good explanation responds to the data rather than defending the original prediction.

body.prompt: A class tests three materials for insulation. Which conclusion uses evidence best?
data: No cover: temperature fell by 18 C
Cotton cover: temperature fell by 10 C
Foam cover: temperature fell by 5 C
choices: Foam was the best insulator because the temperature fell the least, by 5 C.
Cotton is best because it feels soft.
No cover was best because the number 18 is largest.
All materials worked the same because they were tested in the same lesson.

expected_answer.value: Foam was the best insulator because the temperature fell the least, by 5 C.

hints: For insulation, a smaller temperature fall shows less energy transferred by heating.
question_variants.explanation: The foam cover is supported by the evidence because it had the smallest temperature decrease, so it reduced heat transfer most in this test.

body.prompt: Which investigation question is most testable?
choices: How does the amount of light affect the height a bean seedling grows in two weeks?

expected_answer.value: How does the amount of light affect the height a bean seedling grows in two weeks?

hints: Look for something that can be changed and something that can be measured.
Opinions are not good fair-test questions.
question_variants.explanation: The light question is testable because the amount of light can be changed and seedling height can be measured over a set time.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks a fair test means everything stays the same, including the variable being tested.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner locks every variable or refuses to change the independent variable.
repair_sequence: Show one change card unlocked and all control cards locked.
Ask which result would be measured after changing that one thing.
Rebuild the plan using the sentence: I change..., I measure..., I keep...
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner identifies one independent variable and at least two control variables.

misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner places the measurement as the independent variable or the changed condition as dependent.
repair_sequence: Use the change-measure test for each card.
Highlight the independent variable as the input and the dependent variable as the output.
Ask learner to explain the variables in a new context.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner correctly states what is changed and what is measured.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Writes conclusions from guesses or preferences rather than evidence.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses a conclusion without data or says the original prediction must be right.
repair_sequence: Highlight the relevant measurements in the table.
Compare a guess-based conclusion with an evidence-linked conclusion.
Ask learner to write: The evidence shows... because...
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner cites a measurement or observation in the conclusion.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a quiet planner workspace with no timers, flashing or competitive scoring.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Reveal planner cards and data tables instantly or step-by-step without animated movement.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read investigation questions, variable cards and data rows aloud with replay controls.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use sentence frames, icons for change-measure-keep, glossary chips and short method strips.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Show one planner decision at a time and park extra cards in a collapsed tray.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited planning time, undo, printable summaries and side-by-side prediction/data/conclusion views.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Normalise unsupported predictions as useful evidence and separate planning feedback from correctness judgement.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach variable, change, measure, control, prediction, observation, evidence, conclusion, reliable and repeat.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires identifying variables and building a fair investigation plan; secure requires using observations or measurements to decide whether a prediction is supported and to write an evidence-linked conclusion.
evidence.parent_summary: Can plan a fair investigation, choose variables and explain conclusions using evidence.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Identifies change, measure and keep-same variables; orders safe method steps; uses data rather than guesses in prediction-observation-explanation and conclusions.
next_actions: Connect variable planning to graph choice and axes in maths and science.
Apply the planner to energy, forces, cells and particles investigations.
Introduce reliability, repeat means and anomaly handling once basic evidence links are secure.

qa.notes: Review-status Year 7 working-scientifically sample with investigation method planner, POE cycles, evidence-linked conclusions and SEND-safe low-pressure interactions.
